_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how language changes as situations become more formal and as the social distance between people increases.

_source.w: We are learning how language changes as situations become more formal and as the social distance between people increases.
y: Understanding formality helps us communicate appropriately in every context, from casual to highly formal.
Being able to control our register is a sophisticated social and communication skill.
This awareness is essential for professional, academic and civic communication.
eg: We analyse how the language in a legal document differs from a casual text message.
We compare the levels of formality in different types of letters — a letter to a friend versus a letter to a Member of Parliament.
We rewrite a casual message in formal language and discuss what changes we had to make.
We discuss how social distance affects language — we speak differently to close friends than to strangers.
We examine how formality varies across cultures and contexts.
We practise adjusting our register for different audiences in both spoken and written tasks.
